mechanism_narrative: Declining short-term policy rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in the federal funds rate generates positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand. This negative labor shock triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery. The joint positive momentum in consumer spending and residential construction drives equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnation in the index of industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints. When industrial output growth falls below a critical threshold, corporate earnings expectations are downgraded, leading to negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of low-growth industrial production months, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with persistently low policy rates creates a negative real interest rate environment that eventually triggers central bank tightening fears. This divergence forces equity repricing, generating negative returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate above 50%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending. This positive employment trend provides a stable earnings floor, generating positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion. When GDP growth exceeds a sustainable threshold, equity markets price in higher earnings, producing positive returns over one month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in > 60% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production trends and consumer price inflation reveals underlying supply-demand imbalances. When industrial output expands faster than inflation, profit margins improve, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate analysis, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 65% of divergence periods.
